messages.content:
Here are the available tools I can use to assist with Cisco Firepower Management Center (FMC) configuration tasks:

1. functions.lookup_fmc_schema
 • Purpose: Retrieve FMC REST API schema information for a specific configuration type or concept (e.g., subinterfaces, OSPFv2 policies, BGP settings, security zones).
 • Use: To understand required fields, data types, enums, and constraints before generating configurations.

2. functions.validate_fmc_config
 • Purpose: Validate a generated FMC configuration YAML against the API schema.
 • Use: To ensure that the configuration is correct and complete before it is loaded. Returns validation errors if any fields are invalid or missing.

3. functions.load_config_to_ui
 • Purpose: Load a validated FMC configuration YAML into the Device Configuration section of the UI.
 • Use: To parse and display the configuration with counts for each type after validation.

4. multi_tool_use.parallel
 • Purpose: Run multiple tools simultaneously when parallel operation is possible.
 • Use: For efficiency, such as looking up multiple schemas or validating multiple configurations in one step.

Let me know what you would like to do next!
create 5 security zones named happy_1 through happy_5

{"success":true,"result":"FMC API Schema: SecurityZoneObject
Config type: security_zones
Description: A model representing zone object definition.
Type: object
Properties:
  - description: string — User provided resource description.
  - devices: array of IInterfaceDeviceModel
  - id: string — Unique identifier of response object.
  - interfaceMode: string enum=['PASSIVE', 'INLINE', 'SWITCHED', 'ROUTED', 'ASA']
  - interfaces: array of IFTDInterface
  - links: object — This defines the self referencing links for the given resource.
  - metadata: object
  - name: string — User chosen resource name.
  - overridable: boolean — Boolean indicating whether object values can be overridden.
  - overrides: object — Defines the override details for this object.
  - subType: string
  - type: string — Type associated with resource: SecurityZone.
  - version: string — Version number of the response object.

---

FMC API Schema: ISecurityZoneContainer
Config type: security_zones
Type: object
Properties:
  - objects: array of ISecurityZoneObjectModel

---

FMC API Schema: ISecurityZoneObjectModel
Config type: security_zones
Type: object
Properties:
  - description: string
  - devices: array of IInterfaceDeviceModel
  - id: string
  - interfaceMode: string enum=['PASSIVE', 'INLINE', 'SWITCHED', 'ROUTED', 'ASA']
  - interfaces: array of IFTDInterface
  - links: object — This defines the self referencing links for the given resource.
  - metadata: object
  - name: string
  - overridable: boolean
  - overrides: object — Defines the override details for this object.
  - subType: string
  - type: string
  - version: string

---

FMC API Schema: ECMPZoneModel
Config type: ecmp_zones
Description: Equal-Cost Multi-Path (ECMP)
Type: object
Properties:
  - description: string — Description of Equal-Cost Multi-Path (ECMP) Zone
  - id: string — Unique identifier (UUID) representing the ECMP Zone
  - interfaces: array of IFTDInterface
  - links: object — This defines the self referencing links for the given resource.
  - metadata: object — Defines read only details about the object - whether it is system defined, last user who modified the object etc.
  - name: string — ECMP Zone Name
  - type: string — Type of the ECMP Zone. This value is always ecmpzones
  - version: string — Version number of the response object

---

FMC API Schema: CellularInterface
Config type: interfaces
Description: CellularInterface model for managing cellular configuration on FTD devices with cellular capabilities. This API allows configuration of SIM slot settings, data profiles, and provides read-only cellular metadata information.
Type: object
Properties:
  - activeMACAddress: string
  - applicationMonitoring: object
  - arpConfig: array of IARPConfigContainer
  - description: string — User-provided description for the cellular interface.
  - enableAntiSpoofing: boolean — Boolean value indicating whether Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) is enabled on this interface.
  - enableDNSLookup: boolean — Boolean value indicating whether DNS lookups can be performed through this interface.
  - enableSGTPropagate: boolean
  - enabled: boolean — Specifies whether the cellular interface is administratively enabled (true) or disabled (false).
  - fmcAccessConfig: object
  - fragmentReassembly: boolean
  - id: string — Unique identifier (UUID) representing the device.
  - ifname: string — Represents ifname of cellular interface.
  - ipv4: object
  - ipv6: object
  - links: object — This defines the self referencing links for the given resource.
  - macAddressPool: object — Contains reference information.
  - macLearn: boolean
  - macTable: array of string
  - managementOnly: boolean
  - metadata: object
  - mode: string enum=['INLINE', 'PASSIVE', 'TAP', 'ERSPAN', 'NONE', 'SWITCHPORT'] — The operational mode of the interface. For a cellular interface, this must be NONE.
  - name: string — Represents name of cellular interface.
  - overrideDefaultFragmentSetting: object
  - pathMonitoring: object
  - priority: integer — A numeric priority for the cellular interface, used for failover or selection order. A lower number typically means high
  - securityZone: object
  - standbyMACAddress: string
  - type: string — Type of the object; this value is always 'CellularInterface'.
  - version: string

---

FMC API Schema: FPLogicalInterface
Config type: interfaces
Type: object
Properties:
  - description: string
  - enabled: integer
  - icmpEnabled: integer
  - id: string
  - interfaceType: string enum=['SWITCHED', 'ROUTED', 'VLAN']
  - ipAddresses: array of string
  - links: object
  - mdi: string
  - metadata: object
  - mode: string
  - mtu: integer
  - name: string
  - physicalInterface: object
  - securityZone: object
  - staticArpEntries: array of IStaticArpEntry
  - type: string
  - version: string
  - virtualSwitch: object
  - vlanTag: integer"}

To create security zones, I need to know the interface mode for each zone. The interfaceMode field is required and must be one of: PASSIVE, INLINE, SWITCHED, ROUTED, or ASA.

Could you specify which interface mode you want for the happy_1 through happy_5 security zones? If you’re unsure, ROUTED is the most common choice.

function.name: validate_fmc_config
function.arguments:
{"config_yaml":"objects:
  interface:
    security_zones:
      - name: happy_1
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_2
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_3
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_4
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_5
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
"}

messages.content:
Here are the 5 security zones named happy_1 through happy_5 in ROUTED mode, fully validated and ready to load:

```yaml
objects:
  interface:
    security_zones:
      - name: happy_1
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_2
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_3
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_4
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_5
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
```

Would you like to load this configuration into the Device Configuration section?

function.name: load_config_to_ui
function.arguments:
{"config_yaml":"objects:
  interface:
    security_zones:
      - name: happy_1
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_2
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_3
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_4
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
      - name: happy_5
        interfaceMode: ROUTED
        type: SecurityZone
","filename":"happy_security_zones.yaml"}

The configuration for the 5 security zones (happy_1 through happy_5, ROUTED mode) has been loaded into the Device Configuration section and is ready for use. If you need to add interfaces, routing, or other objects, let me know how you'd like to proceed!
